description:
REQ-030 Phase 3, items 6-8: Add a heartbeat system where the daemon reads a user-editable .ao/proactive.md file and evaluates its contents with a lightweight agent.

Changes needed:
1. Add `heartbeat_interval_secs: Option<u64>` to DaemonProjectConfig (default: 1800 = 30min)
2. Add `heartbeat_enabled: bool` to DaemonProjectConfig (default: false — opt-in)
3. In project_tick(), add heartbeat check:
   - If .ao/proactive.md exists AND heartbeat is enabled AND sufficient time elapsed since last check
   - Read file contents
   - Build a context summary: current task stats, stale tasks, recent activity, git state
   - Spawn a lightweight agent (reuse phase executor machinery) with the heartbeat file as instructions + context
4. Silent-OK pattern: if agent responds with no actionable findings, log at debug level and move on
5. If agent finds actionable items, emit them as `proactive-suggestion` daemon events (flows through notifications)
6. Add `ao daemon config set --heartbeat-enabled true --heartbeat-interval-secs 1800`
7. Token budget: add `heartbeat_max_tokens: Option<u32>` to cap context injected into heartbeat agent
8. Add .ao/proactive.md template generation: `ao daemon heartbeat init` creates a starter template
9. Add tests: heartbeat fires at interval, skipped when file absent, skipped when disabled, respects active_hours gating

Example .ao/proactive.md:
```markdown
## Monitoring Instructions
- Check if any tasks have been in-progress for more than 48 hours
- Look for PRs that haven't been reviewed
- Check if cargo test passes on the current main branch
- Flag any requirements in draft status that have all linked tasks done
```

Key files:
- crates/orchestrator-core/src/daemon_config.rs (config fields)
- crates/orchestrator-cli/src/services/runtime/runtime_daemon/daemon_scheduler_project_tick.rs (tick integration)
- New file: daemon_heartbeat.rs (heartbeat logic)
title: Implement .ao/proactive.md heartbeat reader and agent evaluation
